front: Film: psychologist Kris Kelvin travels to a space station orbiting the ocean planet Solaris and is visited by apparitions of his dead wife Hari; adapted from Stanislaw Lem's novel

Film: a committee examines a man who claims to have seen the gigantic face of a child in the ocean; ends with the reveal that Kris Kelvin is on an island in the planet; features close-ups of Brueghel's Hunters in the Snow
Film: a Stalker guides a Writer and a Professor through the Zone (entering via 'the meat grinder' tunnel) to a wish-granting Room; adapted from the Strugatsky brothers' Roadside Picnic
Film: Porcupine committed suicide after wishing for wealth instead of resurrecting his dead brother; a character puts a crown of thorns on his head upon reaching the Room
Film: score features a theme for flute and tar evoking 'space frozen in dynamic equilibrium'; transitions from sepia to color when men arrive at a field of abandoned military equipment

Film: historical epic about 15th-century icon painter Andrei Rublev; divided into eight parts; shot in black-and-white but ends with a color shot of horses grazing; Rublev is inspired to resume painting after watching a bell cast and rung; horse falls down stairs and is impaled on a spear

Film: autobiographical film; Arseny Tarkovsky narrates his own poetry in voiceover; levitation scene; close-ups of Brueghel's Hunters in the Snow with Bach; newsreel from Spanish Civil War interspersed with Ignat reading a letter
Film: nine-minute-long take of a man trying to cross a dry swimming pool without extinguishing a candle; ends with a man and dog lying in a ruined cathedral; made after the director moved to Italy

Film: final film; ends with 'In the beginning was the Word' spoken by the silent 'Little Man'; opens with Alexander planting a tree with his son in a nine-and-a-half minute tracking shot; cinematography by Sven Nykvist
Film: debut feature about a child Red Army scout; ends with the reveal that Ivan was captured and hanged by the Nazis; influenced Parajanov to abandon social realism
Director: his father Arseny was a poet whose verse appears in voiceover in Mirror and Stalker; Arseny had a romance with Marina Tsvetaeva
Director: wrote Sculpting in Time, theorizing the 'time pressure' of long takes in opposition to Soviet montage theory; sided with André Bazin against montage
